MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-23-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT FINDS PLAINTIFF MALCOM FONG'S MOTION TO ENFORCE COURT ORDER AND TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS HAS NOW CONCLUDED. ANY FURTHER RELIEF REQUESTED BY THE PLAINTIFF, AND ANY SANCTIONS REQUESTED BY PLAINTIFF MAY BE TAKEN UP WITH THE TRIAL JUDGE. REGARDING THE DEPOSITION OF SHANNON CRITTENDEN, PLAINTIFF MAY REQUEST OF DEPT. 206 THAT THIS CASE NOT BE ASSIGNED OUT TO TRIAL ON 3/28/16 IN ORDER FOR PARTIES TO HAVE TIME TO TAKE THE DEPOSITION OF MS. CRITTENDEN HERE IN SAN FRANCISCO. IF THE CASE IS ASSIGNED OUT TO TRIAL ON 3/28/16, PLAINTIFF MAY ASK THE TRIAL JUDGE FOR TIME OFF TO TAKE THE DEPOSITION OF MS. CRITTENDEN. PLAINTIFF MAY INFORM DEPT. 206 OR THE TRIAL JUDGE THAT THESE REQUESTS ARE MADE AT THE SUGGESTION OF JUDGE KAHN AS THE EASIEST METHOD FOR TAKING MS. CRITTENDEN'S DEPOSITION. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Enforce Court Order And To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOSHUA D. WHITE, ESQ. of Jones Clifford, Tel No. 415-431-5310 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. PHILLIP T.S. TUKIA, ESQ. of Maranga Morgenstern APLC, Tel No. 415-248-5315 appearing for Defendant Shannon A. Crittenden.

The Court's tentative ruling required parties to appear. Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court finds PLAINTIFF MALCOM FONG'S Motion To Enforce Court Order And To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions has now concluded. Any further relief requested by the Plaintiff, and any sanctions requested by Plaintiff may be taken up with the trial judge. Regarding the deposition of Shannon Crittenden, Plaintiff may request of Dept. 206 that this case not be assigned out to trial on 3/28/16 in order for parties to have time to take the deposition of Ms. Crittenden here in San Francisco. If the case is assigned out to trial on 3/28/16, Plaintiff may ask the trial judge for time off to take the deposition of Ms. Crittenden. Plaintiff may inform Dept. 206 or the trial judge that these requests are made at the suggestion of Judge Kahn as the easiest method for taking Ms. Crittenden's deposition. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
